entries.seoTitle: Football Card Grading ROI Guide — When to Grade | CardSnap
entries.seoDescription: Should grade-or-skip Football cards? See ROI by condition/era/player.
entries.h1: Should You Grade Your Football Card?
entries.subtitle: This guide helps Football card collectors determine when to grade their cards for maximum ROI. Explore the factors influencing grading decisions tailored to Football cards.
gradingLogic: High-value rookie cards from star players are often worth grading due to their potential market demand.
Limited edition or short print cards can significantly appreciate in value when graded, especially in pristine condition.
Vintage Football cards from the 1980s and 1990s can see substantial ROI when graded, particularly if they are in high grade.
Autographed Football cards increase in desirability and value when graded, especially if they are authenticated.
Cards from recent Super Bowl MVPs or Hall of Famers often yield better returns when graded, reflecting their popularity.

keyCharacteristics.title: Centering
keyCharacteristics.desc: Proper centering is crucial for Football cards, as it greatly affects the overall grade and market value.

keyCharacteristics.title: Corners
keyCharacteristics.desc: Sharp corners are essential for grading; any wear can drastically lower the card's value.

keyCharacteristics.title: Surface Quality
keyCharacteristics.desc: Scratches or blemishes on the surface can diminish a Football card's grade and appeal.

keyCharacteristics.title: Edges
keyCharacteristics.desc: Clean, undamaged edges are vital for achieving higher grades, influencing potential resale values.

roiExamples.reason: This card has high demand due to Herbert's performance and potential, making grading a lucrative option.
While this card has some value, the grading cost reduces the ROI potential significantly.
The low value of this common card makes grading financially unviable.

whenToGrade: When you possess a high-demand rookie card from a current star player.
If you have a limited edition card that is in excellent condition.
For vintage Football cards that are well-preserved and have a strong collector interest.
When you own autographed cards that can be authenticated and graded.
If your card features a player who has recently achieved significant accolades, such as a Super Bowl MVP.
skipGrading: When the card is a common base card with little market interest.
If the card is in poor condition with visible wear and tear.
For cards that are not from a recognized brand or set.
When the grading fees exceed the potential market value of the card.
entries.marketInsight: The Football card market has seen a resurgence, with increased demand for both vintage and modern cards. Grading can significantly impact ROI, especially for high-profile players, but collectors should be discerning about which cards to submit.
